_comment: The ONLY Rust coverage exclusions approved by the user (.orchestration/state.md, 2026-07-19).
No executor may add an entry here; propose, do not apply.
Approval (a): crates/chancela-smartcard/src/pkcs11.rs, the cryptoki-CALLING functions only.
The approval is deliberately narrower than the file, so this list is per-function rather than
a filename regex: default_module_path(), resolve_module_path(), resolve_module_path_from(),
key_label_for(), pkcs11_err() and map_login_error() are pure, are unit-tested, and STAY in the
numerator. Excluding pkcs11.rs wholesale would have quietly taken them out too.
Approval (b) — spec requirements blocked on external parties — is not a coverage exclusion.
It belongs to the spec-coverage tracker, is reported as its own BLOCKED row with a named
blocker per requirement, and never enters this numerator.

functions.name: open
functions.reason: calls Cryptoki::new / initialize against a real PKCS#11 module

functions.name: find_key
functions.reason: searches objects on a live token

functions.name: list_certificates
functions.reason: enumerates certificates on a live token

functions.name: sign_digest_with_pin
functions.reason: logs in and signs on a live token
